Name: curvature-aware_precision_routing
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Routing: Deterministic Mixed-Precision Inference via Hessian Conditioning
Short Hypothesis: Precision requirements in neural networks can be dynamically allocated based on local curvature properties, quantified by Hessian conditioning and feature entanglement, enabling deterministic precision routing without training.
Related Work: Existing methods like Spectral Quantization Routing (SQR) use activation geometry for precision allocation, while Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) restricts test-time optimization to low-rank subspaces. This proposal differs by leveraging Hessian conditioning and feature entanglement for deterministic precision routing, avoiding reliance on activation geometry or low-rank projections.
Abstract: Mixed-precision inference offers significant computational savings but often relies on heuristic or static precision allocation strategies. We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Routing (CAPR), a deterministic framework that dynamically allocates numerical precision based on local curvature properties. By quantifying Hessian conditioning and feature entanglement, CAPR identifies regions robust to low-precision computation and those requiring higher precision. We formalize this routing mechanism using PAC-Bayes generalization bounds, linking curvature properties to quantization stability. CAPR computes these diagnostics via deterministic spectral estimators, ensuring strict computational overhead bounds. Evaluated on single-GPU setups across diverse benchmarks, CAPR achieves superior throughput-accuracy trade-offs compared to static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines. By replacing heuristic precision allocation with curvature-aware deterministic routing, CAPR provides a transparent, reproducible pathway for resource-efficient inference.
Experiments: Evaluate CAPR on single-GPU setups across diverse benchmarks (e.g., WILDS, GLUE) to measure throughput-accuracy trade-offs.
Compare CAPR against static and heuristic mixed-precision baselines using metrics like throughput, accuracy, and computational overhead.
Analyze Hessian conditioning and feature entanglement diagnostics to validate precision routing decisions.
Conduct ablation studies to isolate the impact of curvature-aware routing versus other components.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential overhead from Hessian conditioning diagnostics may offset precision routing benefits.
Theoretical bounds may not fully capture practical quantization stability in all scenarios.
Single-GPU validation limits scalability insights for multi-GPU setups.
